headline: Iran Retains Hormuz Leverage Despite Deal, Threatening Cloud Supply Chains
summary: Despite Trump declaring a diplomatic victory, Iran maintains credible capacity to disrupt Strait of Hormuz shipping, through which ~20% of global oil transits. The unresolved tension leaves energy markets volatile and undersea cable routes in the Gulf region exposed to potential interdiction or sabotage. For hyperscale infrastructure operators, sustained instability in this corridor elevates both energy cost unpredictability and physical risk to Middle East data center expansion plans.
oci_implication: OCI's sovereign cloud buildouts in the Gulf region—including UAE and Saudi Arabia—face elevated physical security and energy supply risk if Hormuz tensions escalate; resilience planning for Middle East regions should account for multi-vector disruption scenarios, and sovereign customers in the region will increasingly scrutinize provider continuity guarantees.
